response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with a genuine interest in developer tooling — including direct IDE development experience on NI LabWindows/CVI and several self-built testing/tooling projects — but the core stack requirements are not met. His career centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms, with only incidental Java exposure, no Kotlin, no Spring or enterprise JVM framework experience, and no evidence of deep JVM internals knowledge, which are hard requirements for this role.
strengths: Hands-on IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) and authored developer tooling (test runner, assertion library)
Proven ability to work on complex, high-scale systems (millions of requests/sec, low-latency services)
Strong craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, workshops, and long-term open-source maintenance
concerns: No meaningful Java/Kotlin depth — only brief, secondary Java usage; Kotlin absent entirely
No Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or enterprise JVM framework experience
Recent work (TS/Node, AI tooling, pricing platforms) is far from JVM/IDE domain, suggesting a steep ramp-up
